---
title: "Global RHCC Indicator Dashboard (GRID)"
date: '`r format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%b-%d")`'
output:  
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: scroll
---

```{r setup, include=F}
pacman::p_load(tidyverse,knitr,flexdashboard,plotly,kableExtra,sf,wesanderson,PATHtools,readxl)

setwd("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/") #UPDATE HERE AND FIRST CHUNK FOR EACH COUNTRY TAB. CONTROL F!

#Set global options for figure/table placement
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
	echo = F,
	message = FALSE,
	warning = FALSE,
	dpi = 150,
	results = "asis"
)
options(knitr.kable.NA = '')

#Load data
dat = read_rds("MNTD/Projects/RHCC (Rotary)/Dashboard/data/clean/dat.rds")

palette = c("DRC" = "#FF0000", "MOZ" = "#00A08A", "NGA" = "#F2AD00", "ZAM" = "#5BBCD6")

line_plot = function(dat, date, kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(dat %>% filter(!is.na({{kpi}}))) + 
    geom_line(aes(x = date, y = {{kpi}}, color = adm0), size = 1.2) +
    geom_point(aes(x = date, y = {{kpi}}, color = adm0), size = 1.2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(color = "Country", x ="Date", y = ylab) +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", date_labels =  "%m/%Y")
}

bar_plot = function(dat, kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}})) %>%
           group_by(adm0) %>%
           filter(row_number()==n())) + 
    geom_col(aes(y = {{kpi}}, x = adm0, fill = adm0), 
             position = position_dodge2()) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(fill = "Country", x ="Country", y = ylab) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
    theme_minimal()
}

bar_time_plot = function(dat, kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(data = dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}})) %>%
           group_by(adm0, date = quarter(date, type = "date_first")) %>%
           mutate(kpi = mean({{kpi}}, na.rm = T) %>% round()), 
       aes(x = date, y = kpi, fill = adm0)) +
    geom_bar(position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"), stat = "identity") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(palette)) +
    labs(x ="Date", y = ylab, fill = "Country") +
    scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
    ylim(c(0,100)) +
    theme_minimal()
}

horizontal_bar_plot = function(dat, kpi, lab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}})) %>%
           group_by(adm0) %>%
           filter(row_number()==n())) + 
    geom_col(aes(x = {{kpi}}, y = adm0, fill = adm0), 
             position = position_dodge2(), width = 4) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(fill = "Country", x = lab, y = "Country") +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
    theme_minimal()
}

dumbbell_plot = function(dat, kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter({{kpi}}>0) %>%
           group_by(adm0) %>%
           mutate(survey = as.factor(row_number())) %>%
           ungroup()) + 
    geom_point(aes(y = adm0, x = {{kpi}}, color = adm0, alpha = survey), size = 4) +
    geom_line(aes(y = adm0, x = {{kpi}}, group = adm0)) +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("1" = 1,"2" = 0.7,"3" = 0.4)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(color = "Country", x ="%", y = ylab, alpha = "Survey #") +
    theme_minimal() +
    xlim(c(0,100))
}

#Additional notes for consideration: For KPI with different reporting frequencies in different countries, we can potentially aggregate to the least frequent reporting period.
```

# Multi-Country

## Row

### **RHCC1** - Under-5 Mortality per 1,000 Live Births. Estimates are derived from 2024 DHS survey data.

```{r}
ggplotly(horizontal_bar_plot(dat %>% filter(!is.na(adm0)), rhcc1, "Under-5 mortality rate"))
```

### **RHCC1.1** - Under-5 *Malaria* Hospitalizations per 1,000

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat %>% filter(date>"2025-01-01"), date, rhcc1.1_m, "Under-5 malaria hospitalizations per 1,000 children under 5"))
```

## Row

### **RHCC2** - % of *Malaria* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc2_m, "% of malaria cases managed in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC2** - % of *Pneumonia* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc2_p, "% of pneumonia cases managed in community")+
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC2** - % of *Diarrhoea* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc2_d, "% of diarrhoea cases managed in community")+
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

## Row {data-height="90"}

**The cumulative number of CHWs trained to provide iCCM services, as well as those providing non-curative services in Nigeria, are shown against RHCC targets. The cumulative number of CHWs providing iCCM services who were equipped (bicycle, backpack, registers, etc.) according to the national guidelines are also shown. CHWs who provided non-curative services were trained in Mozambique and Nigeria only (see country tabs).**

## Row

### **RHCC3** - \# of CHWS Trained in iCCM

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(dat %>%
            mutate(rhcc3_clinical_f = ifelse(adm0=="NGA", rhcc3_clinical_f + rhcc3_nonclinical_f, rhcc3_clinical_f),
                   rhcc3_clinical_m = ifelse(adm0=="NGA", rhcc3_clinical_m + rhcc3_nonclinical_m, rhcc3_clinical_m),
                   `RHCC Target` = ifelse(adm0=="NGA", `RHCC Target` + `RHCC Target (other)`, `RHCC Target`)) %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc3_clinical_f)|!is.na(rhcc3_clinical_m)|!is.na(rhcc3_clinical_u)) %>%
            group_by(adm0) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            ungroup() %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc3_clinical_f, rhcc3_clinical_m, rhcc3_clinical_u), names_to = "CHW", names_prefix = "rhcc3_clinical_", values_to = "rhcc3") %>%
            mutate(CHW = factor(CHW, levels = c("f", "m", "u"), labels = c("Female", "Male", "Unknown")))) + 
    geom_bar(data = . %>% filter(CHW %in% c("Female", "Male"), adm0!="MOZ"),
             aes(y = rhcc3, x = adm0, fill = adm0, alpha = CHW),
                 position = "stack", stat = "identity", show.legend = F) +
    geom_bar(data = . %>% filter(CHW=="Unknown", adm0=="MOZ"),
             aes(y = rhcc3, x = adm0, fill = adm0, alpha = CHW),
                 position = "stack", stat = "identity", show.legend = F) +
    geom_point(aes(y = `RHCC Target`, x = adm0, fill = adm0, alpha = "Target"), color = NA) + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(palette)) +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Female" = 1, "Male" = 0.7, "Unknown" = 1)) +
    labs(fill = "Country", x ="Country", y = "# CHWs trained", alpha = "CHW gender") +
    theme_minimal()
)
```

### **RHCC4** - \# of CHWs Equipped (Bicycle, Backpack, Registers, etc.) According to National Guidelines

```{r}
ggplotly(ggplot(dat %>%
                  dplyr::select(-rhcc4) %>%
                  group_by(adm0) %>%
                  filter(!is.na(rhcc4_f)|!is.na(rhcc4_m)|!is.na(rhcc4_u)) %>%
                  filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
                  ungroup() %>%
                  pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc4_f, rhcc4_m, rhcc4_u), names_to = "CHW", names_prefix = "rhcc4_", values_to = "rhcc4") %>%
            mutate(CHW = factor(CHW, levels = c("f", "m", "u"), labels = c("Female", "Male", "Unknown")))) + 
    geom_bar(data = . %>% filter(CHW %in% c("Female", "Male"), adm0!="DRC"),
             aes(y = rhcc4, x = adm0, fill = adm0, alpha = CHW),
                 position = "stack", stat = "identity", show.legend = F) +
    geom_bar(data = . %>% filter(CHW=="Unknown", adm0=="DRC"),
             aes(y = rhcc4, x = adm0, fill = adm0, alpha = CHW),
                 position = "stack", stat = "identity", show.legend = F) +
    geom_point(aes(y = `RHCC Target`, x = adm0, fill = adm0, alpha = "Target"), color = NA) + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(palette)) +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Female" = 1, "Unknown" = 1, "Male" = 0.7)) +
    labs(fill = "Country", x ="Country", y = "Total CHWs equipped", alpha = "CHW gender") +
    theme_minimal())
```

### **RHCC5** - \# of Clinical CHWs Trained and Deployed for iCCM per 1,000 children under-five in RHCC areas. Cumulative figures are shown.

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc5, "# of CHWs per 1,000 children under 5") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,8), expand = c(0,0)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC6** - \# of CHW Supervisors Trained. Cumulative figures are shown.

```{r}
ggplotly(ggplot(dat %>%
                  filter(!is.na(rhcc6_f)|!is.na(rhcc6_m)|!is.na(rhcc6_u)) %>%
                  group_by(adm0) %>%
                  filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
                  ungroup() %>%
                  pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc6_f, rhcc6_m, rhcc6_u), names_to = "Supervisor", names_prefix = "rhcc6_", values_to = "rhcc6") %>%
            mutate(Supervisor = factor(Supervisor, levels = c("f", "m", "u"), 
                                       labels = c("Female", "Male", "Unknown")))) + 
    geom_bar(data = . %>% filter(Supervisor %in% c("Female", "Male"), adm0!="ZAM"),
             aes(y = rhcc6, x = adm0, fill = adm0, alpha = Supervisor),
                 position = "stack", stat = "identity", show.legend = F) +
    geom_bar(data = . %>% filter(Supervisor=="Unknown", adm0=="ZAM"),
             aes(y = rhcc6, x = adm0, fill = adm0, alpha = Supervisor), 
             position = "stack", stat = "identity", show.legend = F) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(palette)) +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Female" = 1, "Male" = 0.7, "Unknown" = 1)) +
    labs(x ="Country", y = "Total Supervisors Trained", alpha = "Supervisor gender",fill = "Country") +
    theme_minimal())
```

### **RHCC7** - % of CHWs Who Received At Least One Supervisory Contact in the Prior Month. Monthly supervision is planned in all countries except **Mozambique, where supervision is quarterly**.

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc7, "% of CHWs with supervisory contact during prior month")+
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC8** - % of CHWs Who Correctly Count Respiratory Rate to Diagnose ARI

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc8, "# of CHWs correctly counting respiratory rate")+
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC9** - % of Sick Child Cases Recommended for Referral by CHW

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat %>% filter(date>"2025-05-01"), date, rhcc9, "% of sick child cases referred")+
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC9.1** - % of Sick Children Referred by CHWs Who are Received at Referral Facility

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc9.1, "% of referrals received")+
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)) +     
           scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month", limits = c(as.Date("2025-12-01"),as.Date("2026-02-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y"))
```

## Row

### **RHCC10** - % of CHWs Trained in iCCM Who Are Providing iCCM 1 Year After Initial Training

```{r}
#ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc10, "% of CHWs providing iCCM 1 year after initial training")+
#           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row

### **RHCC11** - % of Health Facilities with No Stock-outs of Key *Malaria* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc11_m, "% of health facilities with no stock-outs") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC11** - % of Health Facilities with No Stock-outs of Key *Pneumonia* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc11_p, "% of health facilities with no stock-outs") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC11** - % of Health Facilities with No Stock-outs of Key *Diarrhoea* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc11_d, "% of health facilities with no stock-outs") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC12** - % of CHWs with No Stock-outs of Key *Malaria* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc12_m, "% of CHWs with no stock-outs") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC12** - % of CHWs with No Stock-outs of Key *Pneumonia* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc12_p, "% of CHWs with no stock-outs") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC12** - % of CHWs with No Stock-outs of Key *Diarrhoea* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(dat, date, rhcc12_d, "% of CHWs with no stock-outs") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)) +
           scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-10-01"),as.Date("2026-03-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y"))
```

## Row {data-height="30"}

**The percentage of reports submitted on time, the percentage accurate, and the percentage complete during the most recent DQA are shown.**

## Row

### **RHCC13** - % of Reports Submitted on Time

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_time_plot(dat, rhcc13, "% on time"))
```

### **RHCC14** - % of Accurate Reports Submitted

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_time_plot(dat, rhcc14, "% accurate"))
```

### **RHCC15** - % of Complete Reports Submitted

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_time_plot(dat, rhcc15, "% complete"))
```

## Row {data-height="60"}

**Caregivers of children under-5 were surveyed in the community at baseline and endline to ascertain their knowledge of iCCM services, the location of their CHW, and warning signs of childhood illnesses that necessitated immediate medical attention. Unlike in DRC and Nigeria, caregivers who were surveyed in Mozambique and Zambia were surveyed at health facilities.**

## Row

### **RHCC16** - % of Caregivers Aware of iCCM Services

```{r}
ggplotly(dumbbell_plot(dat, rhcc16, "% of caregivers aware of iCCM services"))
```

### **RHCC17** - % of Caregivers Who Know the Location of Their CHW

```{r}
ggplotly(dumbbell_plot(dat, rhcc17, "% of caregivers who know CCM location"))
```

### **RHCC18** - % of Caregivers Who Know Two or More Signs of Childhood Illness That Require Immediate Assessment and, if Appropriate, Treatment

```{r}
ggplotly(dumbbell_plot(dat, rhcc18, "% of caregivers who know warning signs"))
```

## Row

### **RHCC19** - \# of Rotary Volunteers Engaged

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc19, "# of volunteers engaged") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,180), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC20** - \# of Rotary Volunteer Hours Invested in Services, Activities, and Service Projects

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc20, "# of volunteer hours contributed") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,8000), expand = c(0,0)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC21** - \# of Rotary Entities Involved in the Country Program

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc21, "# of Rotary entities involved in the country program") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,15), expand = c(0,0)) +    
           scale_x_discrete(expand = c(1,1))
)
```

### **RHCC22** - \# of Formal Partnerships Established

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc22, "# of formal partnerships established") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,5), expand = c(0,0))
)
```

### **RHCC23** - \# of Advocacy Activities

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc23, "# of advocacy activities") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,10), expand = c(0,0)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC24** - \# of Rotary Members Positioned in Local Decision Making Groups or Advisory Bodies

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc24, "# of Rotary members positioned in local\ndecision-making groups or advisory bodies") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,10), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC25** - \# of Advocacy Activities Covered by Media

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc25, "# of advocacy activities covered by media") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,70), expand = c(0,0)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC26** - \# of Rotary Members Trained on Use of a Community Scorecard

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc26, "# of Rotary members trained on use\nof a Community Scorecard") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,80), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC27** - \# of RHCC Districts Reporting on a Community Scorecard

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc27, "# of districts reporting on a community scorecard") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,10), expand = c(0,0)))
```

# DRC

```{r setup_drc, includ = F}
setwd("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/") #UPDATE HERE AND FIRST CHUNK FOR EACH COUNTRY TAB

#Load data
hz = read_rds("MNTD/Projects/RHCC (Rotary)/Dashboard/data/clean/DRC/hz.rds")
drc = read_rds("MNTD/Projects/RHCC (Rotary)/Dashboard/data/clean/DRC/drc.rds")

palette = c("salmon2","red3","salmon4","darksalmon","red2", "pink2","brown1","red4", "orangered", "orangered3","pink3", "brown2","pink4","brown3","salmon3","salmon1","brown4","pink1")

line_plot = function(kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(data = hz) + 
    geom_line(aes(x = date, y = {{kpi}}, color = `Health Zone`), size = 0.75) +
    geom_point(aes(x = date, y = {{kpi}}, color = `Health Zone`), size = 0.75) +
    scale_color_manual(values = palette) +
    scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month", limits = c(as.Date("2025-12-01"),as.Date("2026-03-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
          plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm")) +
    labs(color = "Health Zone", x ="Date", y = ylab)
}

bar_plot = function(dat, kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}})) %>%
           group_by(`Health Zone`) %>%
           filter(row_number()==n())) + 
    geom_col(aes(x = `Health Zone`, y = {{kpi}}, fill = `Health Zone`), 
             position = position_dodge2()) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(fill = "Health Zone", x = "Health Zone", y = ylab) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45))
}

horizontal_bar_plot = function(dat, kpi, xlab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}})) %>%
           group_by(`Health Zone`) %>%
           filter(row_number()==n())) + 
    geom_col(aes(x = {{kpi}}, y = `Health Zone`, fill = `Health Zone`), 
             position = position_dodge2()) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(fill = "Health Zone", x = xlab, y = "Health Zone") +
    theme_minimal()
}

dumbbell_plot = function(dat, kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}}), {{kpi}}!=0) %>%
           group_by(`Health Zone`) %>%
           mutate(survey = as.factor(row_number())) %>%
           ungroup()) + 
    geom_point(aes(y = `Health Zone`, x = {{kpi}}, color = `Health Zone`, alpha = survey), size = 4) +
    geom_line(aes(y = `Health Zone`, x = {{kpi}}, group = `Health Zone`)) +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("1" = 1,"2" = 0.7,"3" = 0.4)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(fill = "Health Zone", x ="%", y = ylab, alpha = "Survey #", color = "Health Zone") +
    theme_minimal() +
    xlim(c(0,100))
}
```

## Row

### **RHCC1** - Under-5 *Malaria* Mortality per 1,000 Under 5 Population. Under 5 Population is being used as an alternative to the WHO live-births method of calculation to allow for up-to-date burden tracking from monthly surveillance data.

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc1_m, "Under-5 deaths per 1,000 children under 5") +     
           scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", limits = c(as.Date("2021-01-01"),as.Date("2025-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
            ylim(c(0,6))) 
  # |>
  # htmlwidgets::onRender("
  #   function(el) {
  #     el.on('plotly_hover', function(data) {
  #       var pointIndex = data.points[0].pointIndex;
  #       var traceIndex = data.points[0].curveNumber;
  #       var colors = new Array(el.data[0].x.length).fill('#c4c0c2');
  #       colors[pointIndex] = 'gray'; // paint all points gray except the hovered one
  #       Plotly.restyle(el.id, {'marker.color': [colors], 'marker.line.width': 0}, [traceIndex]);
  #     });
  #     el.on('plotly_unhover', function(data) {
  #       // paint all points black
  #       var traceIndex = data.points[0].curveNumber;
  #       Plotly.restyle(el.id, {'marker.color': 'black', 'marker.line.width': 0}, [traceIndex]);
  #     });
  #   }
  # ")
```

## Row 

### **RHCC1** - Under-5 *Pneumonia* Mortality per 1,000 Under 5 Population

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc1_p, "Under-5 deaths per 1,000 children under 5") +     
           scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", limits = c(as.Date("2021-01-01"),as.Date("2025-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
           ylim(c(0,6)))
```

### **RHCC1** - Under-5 *Diarrhoea* Mortality per 1,000 Under 5 Population

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc1_d, "Under-5 deaths per 1,000 children under 5") +     
           scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", limits = c(as.Date("2021-01-01"),as.Date("2025-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
           ylim(c(0,6)))
```
## Row

### **RHCC1.1** - Under-5 *Malaria* Hospitalizations per 1,000 Under 5

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc1.1_m, "Under-5 hospitalizations per 1,000 children under 5") +     
           scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", limits = c(as.Date("2021-01-01"),as.Date("2025-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
           ylim(c(0,200)))
```

### **RHCC1.1** - Under-5 *Pneumonia* Hospitalizations per 1,000 Under 5

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc1.1_p, "Under-5 hospitalizations per 1,000 children under 5") +     
           scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", limits = c(as.Date("2021-01-01"),as.Date("2025-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
           ylim(c(0,200)))
```

### **RHCC1.1** - Under-5 *Diarrhoea* Hospitalizations per 1,000 Under 5

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc1.1_d, "Under-5 hospitalizations per 1,000 children under 5") +     
           scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", limits = c(as.Date("2021-01-01"),as.Date("2025-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
           ylim(c(0,200)))
```
## Row

### **RHCC2** - % of *Malaria* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2_m, "% of malaria cases managed in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC2** - % of *Pneumonia* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2_p, "% of pneumonia cases managed in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC2** - % of *Diarrhoea* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2_d, "% of diarrhoea cases managed in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC2.1** - % of Children with Fever Who Received an RDT in RHCC Communities

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2.1, "% of fever cases receving RDT in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC2.2** - % of Children Testing RDT+ in RHCC Communities

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2.2, "% of fever cases RDT+ in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC2.3** - % of RDT+ Children Who Receive Treatment According to Protocol in RHCC Communities

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2.3, "% of confirmed cases receiving ACT in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC2.4** - % of Children with Diarrhoea Treated with Low Osmolar ORS and Zinc in RHCC Communities

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2.4, "% of diarrhoea cases treated in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC2.5** - % of Children with Pneumonia Treated with Antibiotics (Amoxicillin DT) in RHCC Communities

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2.5, "% of pneumonia cases treated in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC2.6** - % of Cases (Malaria, Pneumonia, Diarrhoea, or Danger Signs) Treated by CHWs During Home Visit According to National Guidelines

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row {data-height="60"}

## Row

### **RHCC3** - \# of CHWS Trained in iCCM.

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(hz %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc3_clinical_f), !is.na(rhcc3_clinical_m)) %>%
            group_by(`Health Zone`) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc3_clinical_f, rhcc3_clinical_m), names_to = "CHW", names_prefix = "rhcc3_clinical_", values_to = "rhcc3") %>%
            mutate(CHW = factor(CHW, levels = c("f", "m"), labels = c("Female", "Male")))) +
    geom_bar(aes(y = rhcc3, x = `Health Zone`, fill = `Health Zone`, alpha = CHW),
             position = "stack", stat = "identity") +
    geom_point(aes(y = rhcc3_target, x = `Health Zone`, fill = `Health Zone`, alpha = "Target"), color = NA) + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(palette)) +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Female" = 1, "Male" = 0.7, "Target" = 0.7)) +
    labs(fill = "Health Zone", x ="Health Zone", y = "# CHWs trained", alpha = "CHW gender") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45))
)
```

## Row 

### **RHCC4** - \# of CHWs Equipped (Bicycle, Backpack, Registers, etc.) According to National Guidelines.

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(hz, rhcc4, "# of CHWs equipped"))
```

### **RHCC5** - \# of Clinical CHWs Trained and Deployed for iCCM per 1,000 children under-five in RHCC areas. Cumulative figures are shown.

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(hz, rhcc5, "# of CHWs per 1,000 children under 5"))
```

## Row

### **RHCC6** - \# of CHW Supervisors Trained. Cumulative figures are shown.

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(hz %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc6_f), !is.na(rhcc6_m)) %>%
            group_by(`Health Zone`) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc6_f, rhcc6_m), names_to = "Gender", names_prefix = "rhcc6_", values_to = "rhcc6") %>%
            mutate(Gender = factor(Gender, levels = c("f", "m"), labels = c("Female", "Male")))) +
    geom_bar(aes(y = rhcc6, x = `Health Zone`, fill = `Health Zone`, alpha = Gender),
             position = "stack", stat = "identity") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(palette)) +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Female" = 1, "Male" = 0.7)) +
    labs(fill = "Health Zone", x ="Health Zone", y = "Total supervisors trained", alpha = "Gender") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45))
)
```

## Row

### **RHCC7** - % of CHWs Who Received At Least One Supervisory Contact in the Prior Month

```{r}
# ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc7, "% of CHWs with supervisory contact during prior month")+
#            scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC8** - % of CHWs Who Correctly Count Respiratory Rate to Diagnose ARI

```{r}
# ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc8, "# of CHWs correctly counting respiratory rate")+
#            scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row

### **RHCC9** - % of Sick Child Cases Recommended for Referral by CHW

```{r}
# ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc9, "% of sick child cases referred") +
#            scale_y_continuous(lim = c(-2,50)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC9.1** - % of Sick Children Referred by CHWs Who Are Received at Referral Facility

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row

### **RHCC10** - % of CHWs Trained in iCCM Who Are Providing iCCM 1 Year After Initial Training

```{r}
#ggplotly(bar_plot(dat_state, rhcc10, "% of CHWs providing iCCM 1 year after initial training")+
 #          scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row

### **RHCC11** - % of Health Facilities with No Stock-outs of Key *Malaria* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
#ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc11_m, "% of health facilities with no stock-outs") +
 #          scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC11** - % of Health Facilities with No Stock-outs of Key *Pneumonia* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
#ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc11_p, "% of health facilities with no stock-outs") +
 #          scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC11** - % of Health Facilities with No Stock-outs of Key *Diarrhoea* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
#ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc11_d, "% of health facilities with no stock-outs") +
 #          scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row

### **RHCC12** - % of CHWs with No Stock-outs of Key *Malaria* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
#ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc12_m, "% of CHWs with no stock-outs") +
#            scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC12** - % of CHWs with No Stock-outs of Key *Pneumonia* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc12_p, "% of CHWs with no stock-outs") +
            scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC12** - % of CHWs with No Stock-outs of Key *Diarrhoea* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc12_d, "% of CHWs with no stock-outs") +
            scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC12.9** - # of expected reports

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC13** - % of Reports Submitted on Time

```{r}
#line_plot(rhcc13, "% on time")
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC14** - % of Accurate Reports Submitted

```{r}
#line_plot(rhcc14, "% on time")
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC15** - % of Complete Reports Submitted

```{r}
#line_plot(rhcc15, "% on time")
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row {data-height="60"}

**Caregivers of children under-5 were surveyed in the community at baseline and endline to ascertain their knowledge of iCCM services, the location of their CHW, and warning signs of childhood illnesses that necessitated immediate medical attention**

## Row

### **RHCC16** - % of Caregivers Aware of iCCM Services

```{r}
ggplotly(dumbbell_plot(hz, rhcc16, "% of caregivers aware of iCCM services"))
```

### **RHCC17** - % of Caregivers Who Know the Location of Their CHW

```{r}
#ggplotly(dumbbell_plot(dat_state, rhcc17, "% of caregivers who know CCM location"))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC18** - % of Caregivers Who Know Two or More Signs of Childhood Illness That Require Immediate Assessment and, if Appropriate, Treatment

```{r}
#ggplotly(dumbbell_plot(dat, rhcc18, "% of caregivers who know warning signs"))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row

### **RHCC19** - \# of Rotary Volunteers Engaged

```{r}
bar_plot = function(dat, kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}})) %>%
           filter(row_number()==n())) + 
    geom_col(aes(y = {{kpi}}, x = adm0, fill = adm0), position = position_dodge2()) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = "#FF0000", guide = F) +
    labs(x ="", y = ylab) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
    scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.5,0.5)) +
    theme_minimal()
}

ggplotly(bar_plot(drc, rhcc19, "# of Rotary volunteers engaged") + ylim(c(0,15)))
```

### **RHCC20** - \# of Rotary Volunteer Hours Invested in Services, Activities, and Service Projects

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(drc, rhcc20, "# of Rotary volunteer hours invested") + ylim(c(0,3000)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC21** - \# of Rotary Entities Involved in the Country Program

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC22** - \# of Formal Partnerships Established

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(drc, rhcc22, "# of formal partnerships established") + ylim(c(0,3)))
```

### **RHCC23** - \# of Advocacy Activities

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(drc, rhcc23, "# of formal partnerships established") + ylim(c(0,5)))
```

## Row 

### **RHCC24** - \# of Rotary Members Positioned in Local Decision Making Groups or Advisory Bodies

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(drc, rhcc24, "# of formal partnerships established") + ylim(c(0,5)))
```

### **RHCC25** - \# of Advocacy Activities Covered by Media

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(drc, rhcc25, "# of advocacy activities covered by media") + ylim(c(0,5)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC26** - \# of Rotary Members Trained on Use of a Community Scorecard

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(drc, rhcc26, "# of Rotary members trained on use of a community scorecard") + ylim(c(0,15)) + theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 9)))
```

### **RHCC27** - \# of RHCC Districts Reporting on a Community Scorecard

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```


## Row

### **RHCC28** - # of Advocacy Sessions Carried Out Nationally to Promote RHCC Sustainability

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC29** - # of Quarterly Monitoring Meetings with Implementing Partners and the Ministry Conducted at National Level

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC30** - # of Quarterly Monitoring Missions Conducted at Provincial Level

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

# Mozambique

```{r}
setwd("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/") #UPDATE HERE AND FIRST CHUNK FOR EACH COUNTRY TAB

setwd("MNTD/Projects/RHCC (Rotary)/Mozambique/")

# Load Data
sheet_names <- excel_sheets("data/Reporte Mensal de KPIs do RHCC.xlsx")
all_sheets_list <- excel_sheets("data/Reporte Mensal de KPIs do RHCC.xlsx") %>%
  set_names() %>%
  map(~ read_excel(path = "data/Reporte Mensal de KPIs do RHCC.xlsx", sheet = .x))

selected_list <- map(all_sheets_list, ~select(.x, `What we are trying to measure`:Pneumonia))


combined_df <- bind_rows(selected_list, .id = "sheet_name_column") %>%
  filter(sheet_name_column != "All_data_wide",
         sheet_name_column != "All_data_long")
  
combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  dplyr::select(sheet_name_column:Pneumonia) %>%
  mutate(period = parse_date_time(sheet_name_column, orders = "b y", quiet = TRUE))

#Bring in the disaggregated district data
district_aps_dat <- read_csv("data/district_aps_raw_data.csv",
                             locale=locale(encoding="latin1")) %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  mutate(period = parse_date_time(mes, orders = "b-y", quiet = TRUE))


#Bring in disaggregated case data
district_cases_dat <- read_csv("data/district_aps_total_cases.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  rename(district = row_labels) %>%
  mutate(period = parse_date_time(month, orders = "y-b", quiet = TRUE))

#palette = wes_palette("Darjeeling1")[c(1:3,5)]
#Plotting functions
pathcolors <- list("DkRed"="#a8001e", "Red"="#f65050", "PtlRed"="#fbb9b9",
                   "DkPurple"="#37379b", "Purple"="#5050cd", "PtlYellow"="#ffe178", 
                   "DkTeal"="#005a87", "Teal"="#008c9b", "PtlTeal"="#99d1d7", 
                   "DkGreen"="#056e23", "Green"="#1eaf5f", "PtlGreen"="#a5dfbf", 
                   "Black"="#000000", "Gray"="#464f60", "LtGray"="#e8eaeb", "Biege"="#f4ede7")

palette = pathcolors[c(1,2,5,6,8)]

cols = c("Chifunde" = "#004B49", "Dôa" = "#00827F", "Manica" = "#48D1CC",
         "Mutarara" = "#01F9C6")
```

## Row 1

### **RHCC1** - Under-5 Mortality per 1,000 Under 5 Population. Under 5 Population is being used as an alternative to the WHO live-births method of calculation to allow for up-to-date burden tracking from monthly surveillance data.

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
rhcc1 <- district_aps_dat %>%
  mutate(distrito = if_else(distrito == "Doa", "Dôa", distrito)) %>%
  mutate(across(-c(distrito:mes, period), as.numeric)) %>%
  dplyr::select(distrito, mes, period, criancas_menores_de_5_anos, criancas_nascidas, 
            mortes_na_comunidade_crianca_5_anos) %>%
  filter(distrito != "District") %>%
  group_by(distrito, year = lubridate::year(period), period) %>%
  summarise(criancas_menores_de_5_anos = mean(criancas_menores_de_5_anos),
            criancas_nascidas = sum(criancas_nascidas, na.rm = TRUE),
            mortes_na_comunidade_crianca_5_anos = sum(mortes_na_comunidade_crianca_5_anos, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(u5_mort_rate = mortes_na_comunidade_crianca_5_anos/criancas_menores_de_5_anos * 1000,
         u5_mort_live_births = mortes_na_comunidade_crianca_5_anos/criancas_nascidas)

rhcc1_mort_plot = rhcc1 %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_col(aes(x = period, y = u5_mort_rate, fill = distrito), position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Under-5 deaths per 1,000 children under 5", fill = "District") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  theme_minimal() #+
  #facet_wrap(vars(as.factor(year))) +
  #coord_flip()

ggplotly(rhcc1_mort_plot)

# ggplot() +
#   annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
#            label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
#            fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
#   theme_void() +
#   
```

## Row 2

### **RHCC1.1** - Under-5 Hospitalizations per 1,000 Under 5 Population

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row 3

### **RHCC2** - % of *Malaria* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
rhcc2 <- district_cases_dat %>%
  mutate(district = if_else(district == "Doa", "Dôa", district)) %>%
  mutate(pct_community_mal = round(malaria_cases_treated_community/all_malaria_cases_treated * 100, 2),
         pct_community_pneum = round(pneumonia_cases_treated_community/all_pneumonia_cases_treated * 100, 2),
         pct_community_diarr = round(diarrhea_cases_treated_community/all_diarrhea_cases_treated * 100, 2))

#Malaria
rhcc2_plot_malaria <- rhcc2 %>%
  filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = pct_community_mal, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "% of malaria cases managed in community", color = "District") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm")) +
  scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0))


ggplotly(rhcc2_plot_malaria)
```

### **RHCC2** - % of *Pneumonia* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
#Pneumonia
rhcc2_plot_pneumonia <- rhcc2 %>%
  filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = pct_community_pneum, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "% of pneumonia cases managed in community", color = "District") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm")) +
  scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0))


ggplotly(rhcc2_plot_pneumonia)
```

### **RHCC2** - % of *Diarrhoea* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
rhcc2_plot_diarrhoea <- rhcc2 %>%
  filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = pct_community_diarr, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "% of diarrhoea cases managed in community", color = "District") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm")) +
  scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0))


ggplotly(rhcc2_plot_diarrhoea)
```

## Row 4

### **RHCC2.2** - Proportion of Children Testing RDT+ in RHCC Communities

```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
rhcc2.2 <- combined_df %>%
  filter(`KPI Unique Identifier` == "RHCC2.2",
         `Numerator/Denominator` == "Total= Num/Den") %>%
  dplyr::select(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, period, Chifunde:Mutarara) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Chifunde:Mutarara), names_to = "district", values_to = "value")

rhcc2.2_plot <- rhcc2.2 %>%
  #filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = value, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Proportion of fever cases RDT+ in community", color = "District") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"))


ggplotly(rhcc2.2_plot)

```

### **RHCC2.3** - Proportion of RDT+ Children Who Receive Treatment According to Protocol in RHCC Communities

```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
rhcc2.3 <- combined_df %>%
  filter(`KPI Unique Identifier` == "RHCC2.3",
         `Numerator/Denominator` == "Total= Num/Den") %>%
  dplyr::select(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, period, Chifunde:Mutarara) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Chifunde:Mutarara), names_to = "district", values_to = "value")

rhcc2.3_plot <- rhcc2.3 %>%
  #filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = value, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Proportion of confirmed cases receiving ACT in community", color = "District") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"))


ggplotly(rhcc2.3_plot)

```

## Row 5

### **RHCC2.4** - Proportion of Children with Diarrhoea Treated with Low Osmolar ORS and Zinc in RHCC Communities

```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
rhcc2.4 <- combined_df %>%
  filter(`KPI Unique Identifier` == "RHCC2.4",
         `Numerator/Denominator` == "Total= Num/Den") %>%
  dplyr::select(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, period, Chifunde:Mutarara) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Chifunde:Mutarara), names_to = "district", values_to = "value")

rhcc2.4_plot <- rhcc2.4 %>%
  #filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = value, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Proportion of diarrhea cases treated in community", color = "District") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"))


ggplotly(rhcc2.4_plot)

```

### **RHCC2.5** - Proportion of Children with Pneumonia Treated with Antibiotics (Amoxicillin) in RHCC Communities

```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
rhcc2.5 <- combined_df %>%
  filter(`KPI Unique Identifier` == "RHCC2.5",
         `Numerator/Denominator` == "Total= Num/Den") %>%
  dplyr::select(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, period, Chifunde:Mutarara) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Chifunde:Mutarara), names_to = "district", values_to = "value")

rhcc2.5_plot <- rhcc2.5 %>%
  #filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = value, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Proportion of pneumonia cases treated in community", color = "District") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"))


ggplotly(rhcc2.5_plot)

```

## Row 6

### **RHCC3** - \# of CHWS Trained in iCCM

```{r}
#Gender disaggregation only in one district and one month
rhcc3 <- combined_df %>%
  filter(`KPI Unique Identifier` == "RHCC3",
         `Numerator/Denominator` == "Total= Sum(num)") %>%
  dplyr::select(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, period, Chifunde:Mutarara) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Chifunde:Mutarara), names_to = "district", values_to = "value") %>%
  group_by(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, district) %>%
  summarise(total_trained = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE))

rhcc3_plot <- rhcc3 %>%
ggplot() +
  geom_col(aes(x = district, y = total_trained, fill = district)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "District", y = "# CHWs trained",
       fill = "District")

ggplotly(rhcc3_plot)
```

### **RHCC4** - % of CHWs Trained That Are Equipped (Bicycle, Backpack, Registers, etc.) According to National Guidelines

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1,
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC5** - \# of CHWs Trained and Deployed for iCCM per 1,000 children under-five in RHCC areas. Cumulative figures are shown.

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1,
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row 7

### **RHCC6** - \# of CHW Supervisors Trained

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1,
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC7** - % of CHWs Who Received At Least One Supervisory Contact in the Prior Month

```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
rhcc7 <- combined_df %>%
  filter(`KPI Unique Identifier` == "RHCC7",
         `Numerator/Denominator` == "Total= Num/Den") %>%
  dplyr::select(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, period, Chifunde:Mutarara) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Chifunde:Mutarara), names_to = "district", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(value = round(value*100))

rhcc7_plot <- rhcc7 %>%
  #filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = value, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "% of CHWs with supervisory contact during prior month", color = "District") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"))


ggplotly(rhcc7_plot)

```

### **RHCC8** - % of CHWs Who Correctly Count Respiratory Rate to Diagnose ARI

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1,
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row 8

### **RHCC9** - % of Sick Child Cases Recommended for Referral by CHW

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1,
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

### **RHCC9.1** - % of Sick Children Referred by CHWs Who Are Received at Referral Facility

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row 9

### **RHCC10 - RHCC12**

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1,
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row 10

### **RHCC13** - % of Reports Submitted on Time

```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
rhcc13 <- combined_df %>%
  filter(`KPI Unique Identifier` == "RHCC13",
         `Numerator/Denominator` == "Total= Num/Den") %>%
  dplyr::select(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, period, Chifunde:Mutarara) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Chifunde:Mutarara), names_to = "district", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(value = round(value*100))

rhcc13_plot <- rhcc13 %>%
  #filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = value, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Proportion on time", color = "District") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  ylim(0, 100) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"))


ggplotly(rhcc13_plot)

```

## Row 11

### **RHCC14 - RHCC18**

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1,
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row 12

### **RHCC19** - \# of Rotary Volunteers Engaged

```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#gender disaggregated numbers don't align with total
rhcc19 <- combined_df %>%
  filter(`KPI Unique Identifier` == "RHCC19") %>%
  dplyr::select(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, period, Chifunde:Mutarara) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Chifunde:Mutarara), names_to = "district", values_to = "value")

rhcc19_plot <- rhcc19 %>%
  #filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_col(aes(x = period, y = value, fill = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "# of volunteers engaged", fill = "District") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  #ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplotly(rhcc19_plot)

```

### **RHCC20** - \# of Rotary Volunteer Hours Invested in Services, Activities, and Service Projects

```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#gender disaggregated numbers don't align with total
rhcc20 <- combined_df %>%
  filter(`KPI Unique Identifier` == "RHCC20") %>%
  dplyr::select(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, period, Chifunde:Mutarara) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Chifunde:Mutarara), names_to = "district", values_to = "value")

rhcc20_plot <- rhcc20 %>%
  #filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_col(aes(x = period, y = value, fill = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "# of volunteer hours contributed", fill = "District") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  #ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplotly(rhcc20_plot)

```

## Row 13

### **RHCC21 - RHCC25**

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1,
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row 14

### **RHCC26** - \# of Rotary Members Trained on Use of a Community Scorecard

```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#gender disaggregated numbers don't align with total
rhcc26 <- combined_df %>%
  filter(`KPI Unique Identifier` == "RHCC26") %>%
  dplyr::select(`Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Across RHCC Countries`, period, Chifunde:Mutarara) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Chifunde:Mutarara), names_to = "district", values_to = "value")

rhcc26_plot <- rhcc26 %>%
  #filter(period <= "2025-12-01") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_col(aes(x = period, y = value, fill = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "# of volunteers  trained on community scorecards", fill = "District") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  #ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal()


ggplotly(rhcc26_plot)

```

### **RHCC27** - \# of RHCC Districts Reporting on a Community Scorecard

```{r}
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1,
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = pathcolors$PtlYellow, color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

# Nigeria

```{r setup_nga, includ = F}
#Load data
setwd("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/") #UPDATE HERE AND FIRST CHUNK FOR EACH COUNTRY TAB

dat_state = read_rds("MNTD/Projects/RHCC (Rotary)/Dashboard/data/clean/Nigeria/dat_state.rds")
dat_adm0 = read_rds("MNTD/Projects/RHCC (Rotary)/Dashboard/data/clean/Nigeria/dat_adm0.rds")

palette = c("orange1", "brown1")

line_plot = function(kpi, ylab){
  ggplot() + 
    geom_line(data = dat_state %>% filter(date>"2025-09-01"), 
              aes(x = date, y = {{kpi}}, color = State), size = 1.5) +
    geom_point(data = dat_state  %>% filter(date>"2025-09-01"), 
               aes(x = date, y = {{kpi}}, color = State), size = 1.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = palette) +
    scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month", limits = c(as.Date("2025-10-01"),as.Date("2026-03-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
          plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm")) +
    labs(color = "State", x ="Date", y = ylab)
}

bar_plot = function(dat, kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}})) %>%
           group_by(State) %>%
           filter(row_number()==n())) + 
    geom_col(aes(x = State, y = {{kpi}}, fill = State), 
             position = position_dodge2()) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(fill = "State", x = "State", y = ylab) +
    theme_minimal()
}

horizontal_bar_plot = function(dat, kpi, xlab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}})) %>%
           group_by(State) %>%
           filter(row_number()==n())) + 
    geom_col(aes(x = {{kpi}}, y = State, fill = State), 
             position = position_dodge2()) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(fill = "State", x = xlab, y = "State") +
    theme_minimal()
}

dumbbell_plot = function(dat, kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}}), {{kpi}}!=0) %>%
           group_by(State) %>%
           mutate(survey = as.factor(row_number())) %>%
           ungroup()) + 
    geom_point(aes(y = State, x = {{kpi}}, color = State, alpha = survey), size = 4) +
    geom_line(aes(y = State, x = {{kpi}}, group = State)) +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("1" = 1,"2" = 0.7,"3" = 0.4)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(fill = "State", x ="%", y = ylab, alpha = "Survey #", color = "State") +
    theme_minimal() +
    xlim(c(0,100))
}
```

## Row

### **RHCC1** - Under-5 Mortality per 1,000 Live Births. Values are from Nigeria DHS 2024.

```{r}
ggplotly(horizontal_bar_plot(dat_state, rhcc1, "Under-5 mortality rate"))
```

## Row

### **RHCC2** - % of *Malaria* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2_m, "% of malaria cases managed in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(-2,100)))
```

### **RHCC2** - % of *Pneumonia* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2_p, "% of pneumonia cases managed in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(-2,100)))
```

### **RHCC2** - % of *Diarrhoea* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2_d, "% of diarrhoea cases managed in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(-2,100)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC2.1** - % of Children with Fever Who Received an RDT in RHCC Communities

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2.1, "% of fever cases receving RDT in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(-2,100)))
```

### **RHCC2.2** - % of Children Testing RDT+ in RHCC Communities

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2.2, "% of fever cases RDT+ in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(-2,100)))
```

### **RHCC2.3** - % of RDT+ Children Who Receive Treatment According to Protocol in RHCC Communities

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2.3, "% of confirmed cases receiving ACT in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(-2,105)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC2.4** - % of Children with Diarrhoea Treated with Low Osmolar ORS and Zinc in RHCC Communities

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2.4, "% of diarrhoea cases treated in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(-2,120)))
```

### **RHCC2.5** - % of Children with Pneumonia Treated with Antibiotics (Amoxicillin DT) in RHCC Communities

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc2.5, "% of pneumonia cases treated in community") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(-2,140)))
```

## Row {data-height="60"}

**The cumulative number of CHWs who were trained to provide iCCM services and non-curative services are shown. The cumulative number of CHWs providing iCCM services who were equipped (bicycle, backpack, registers, etc.) according to national guidelines are also shown.**

## Row

### **RHCC3** - \# of CHWS Trained in iCCM. All CHIPS agents are female and all CEFPs are male.

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(dat_state %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc3_clinical_f), !is.na(rhcc3_clinical_m)) %>%
            group_by(State) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            rename(rhcc3_clinical = rhcc3_clinical_f, rhcc3_nonclinical = rhcc3_nonclinical_m) %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc3_clinical, rhcc3_nonclinical), names_to = "CHW", names_prefix = "rhcc3_", values_to = "rhcc3") %>%
            mutate(CHW = factor(CHW, levels = c("clinical", "nonclinical"), labels = c("CHIPS", "CEFPs")))) +
    geom_bar(aes(y = rhcc3, x = State, fill = State, alpha = CHW),
             position = "stack", stat = "identity") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(palette)) +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("CHIPs" = 1, "CEFPs" = 0.7)) +
    labs(fill = "State", x ="State", y = "# CHWs trained", alpha = "CHW type") +
    theme_minimal()
)
```

### **RHCC4** - \# of CHWs Equipped (Bicycle, Backpack, Registers, etc.) According to National Guidelines. All equipped CHIPS agents are female.

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat_state, rhcc4, "# of CHWs equipped"))
```

### **RHCC5** - \# of Clinical CHWs Trained and Deployed for iCCM per 1,000 children under-five in RHCC areas. Cumulative figures are shown.

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat_state, rhcc5, "# of CHWs per 1,000 children under 5"))
```

## Row

### **RHCC6** - \# of CHW Supervisors Trained. Cumulative figures are shown.

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(dat_state %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc6_f), !is.na(rhcc6_m)) %>%
            group_by(State) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc6_f, rhcc6_m), names_to = "Gender", names_prefix = "rhcc6_", values_to = "rhcc6") %>%
            mutate(Gender = factor(Gender, levels = c("f", "m"), labels = c("Female", "Male")))) +
    geom_bar(aes(y = rhcc6, x = State, fill = State, alpha = Gender),
             position = "stack", stat = "identity") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(palette)) +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Female" = 1, "Male" = 0.7)) +
    labs(fill = "State", x ="State", y = "Total supervisors trained", alpha = "Gender") +
    theme_minimal()
)
```

### **RHCC7** - % of CHWs Who Received At Least One Supervisory Contact in the Prior Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc7, "% of CHWs with supervisory contact during prior month")+
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

### **RHCC8** - % of CHWs Who Correctly Count Respiratory Rate to Diagnose ARI

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc8, "# of CHWs correctly counting respiratory rate")+
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC9** - % of Sick Child Cases Recommended for Referral by CHW

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc9, "% of sick child cases referred") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,20)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC10** - % of CHWs Trained in iCCM Who Are Providing iCCM 1 Year After Initial Training

```{r}
#ggplotly(bar_plot(dat_state, rhcc10, "% of CHWs providing iCCM 1 year after initial training")+
 #          scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row

### **RHCC11** - % of Health Facilities with No Stock-outs of Key *Malaria* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
#ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc11_m, "% of health facilities with no stock-outs") +
 #          scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100), expand = c(0,0)))
ggplot() +
  annotate("label", x = 1, y = 1, 
           label = "Data Collection/Processing Underway",
           fill = "#ffe178", color = "black", size = 6, label.padding = unit(1, "lines")) +
  theme_void()
```

## Row

### **RHCC12** - % of CHWs with No Stock-outs of Key *Malaria* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc12_m, "% of CHWs with no stock-outs") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC12** - % of CHWs with No Stock-outs of Key *Pneumonia* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc12_p, "% of CHWs with no stock-outs") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC12** - % of CHWs with No Stock-outs of Key *Diarrhoea* Medicines and Diagnostics in the Past Month

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc12_d, "% of CHWs with no stock-outs") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

## Row

### **RHCC13** - % of Reports Submitted on Time

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc13, "% on time") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC14** - % of Accurate Reports Submitted

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc14, "% accurate") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

### **RHCC15** - % of Complete Reports Submitted

```{r}
ggplotly(line_plot(rhcc15, "% complete") +
           scale_y_continuous(lim = c(0,100)))
```

## Row {data-height="60"}

**Caregivers of children under-5 were surveyed in the community at baseline and endline to ascertain their knowledge of iCCM services, the location of their CHW, and warning signs of childhood illnesses that necessitated immediate medical attention**

## Row

### **RHCC16** - % of Caregivers Aware of iCCM Services

```{r}
ggplotly(dumbbell_plot(dat_state, rhcc16, "% of caregivers aware of iCCM services"))
```

### **RHCC17** - % of Caregivers Who Know the Location of Their CHW

```{r}
ggplotly(dumbbell_plot(dat_state, rhcc17, "% of caregivers who know CCM location"))
```

### **RHCC18** - % of Caregivers Who Know Two or More Signs of Childhood Illness That Require Immediate Assessment and, if Appropriate, Treatment

```{r}
ggplotly(dumbbell_plot(dat_state, rhcc18, "% of caregivers who know warning signs"))
```

## Row

### **RHCC19** - \# of Rotary Volunteers Engaged

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(dat %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc19_rotarian), !is.na(rhcc19_nonrotarian)) %>%
            group_by(year) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            dplyr::select(year,rhcc19_rotarian,rhcc19_nonrotarian) %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc19_rotarian, rhcc19_nonrotarian), names_to = "Volunteer type", names_prefix = "rhcc19_", values_to = "rhcc19") %>%
            mutate(`Volunteer type` = factor(`Volunteer type`, levels = c("rotarian", "nonrotarian"), labels = c("Rotarian", "Non-Rotarian")))) +
    geom_bar(aes(y = rhcc19, x = year, alpha = `Volunteer type`),
             position = "stack", stat = "identity", fill = "#F2AD00") +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Rotarian" = 1, "Non-Rotarian" = 0.7)) +
    labs(fill = "", x ="Year", y = "# of volunteers engaged", alpha = "Volunteer type") +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.3,0.3), breaks = c(1,2)) +
    theme_minimal()
)
```

### **RHCC20** - \# of Rotary Volunteer Hours Invested in Services, Activities, and Service Projects

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(dat %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc20_advocacy), !is.na(rhcc20_coordination), !is.na(rhcc20_implementation)) %>%
            group_by(year) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            dplyr::select(year,rhcc20_advocacy,rhcc20_coordination,rhcc20_implementation) %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc20_advocacy,rhcc20_coordination,rhcc20_implementation), names_to = "Contribution type", names_prefix = "rhcc20_", values_to = "rhcc20") %>%
            mutate(`Contribution type` = factor(`Contribution type`, levels = c("advocacy", "coordination","implementation"), labels = c("Advocacy", "Coordination","Implementation")))) +
    geom_bar(aes(y = rhcc20, x = year, alpha = `Contribution type`),
             position = "stack", stat = "identity", fill = "#F2AD00") +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Advocacy" = 1, "Coordination" = 0.7, "Implementation" = 0.4)) +
    labs(fill = "", x ="Year", y = "# of volunteer hours invested", alpha = "Contribution type") +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.3,0.3), breaks = c(1,2)) +
    theme_minimal()
)
```

## Row

### **RHCC21** - \# of Rotary Entities Involved in the Country Program

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(dat %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc21_dis), !is.na(rhcc21_clb), !is.na(rhcc21_nnppc), !is.na(rhcc21_ramglobal), !is.na(rhcc21_rfha)) %>%
            group_by(year) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            dplyr::select(year,rhcc21_dis,rhcc21_clb,rhcc21_rfha,rhcc21_ramglobal,rhcc21_nnppc) %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc21_dis,rhcc21_clb,rhcc21_rfha,rhcc21_ramglobal,rhcc21_nnppc), names_to = "Entity type", names_prefix = "rhcc21_", values_to = "rhcc21") %>%
            mutate(`Entity type` = factor(`Entity type`, levels = c("dis","clb", "rfha","ramglobal","nnppc"), labels = c("District","Rotaract club","RFHA","RAM Global","NNPPC")))) +
    geom_bar(aes(y = rhcc21, x = year, alpha = `Entity type`),
             position = "stack", stat = "identity", fill = "#F2AD00") +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("District" = 1, "Rotaract club" = 0.8,"RFHA" = 0.6, "RAM Global" = 0.4, "NNPPC" = 0.2)) +
    labs(fill = "", x ="Year", y = "# of Rotary entities involved in the country program", alpha = "Entity type") +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.3,0.3), breaks = c(1,2)) +
    theme_minimal()
)
```
### **RHCC22** - \# of Formal Partnerships Established

```{r}
bar_plot = function(dat, kpi, ylab){
  ggplot(dat %>%
           filter(!is.na({{kpi}})) %>%
           group_by(year) %>%
           filter(row_number()==n())) + 
    geom_col(aes(y = {{kpi}}, x = year), fill = "#F2AD00", width = 1) +
    labs(x ="Year", y = ylab) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.3,0.3), breaks = c(1,2)) +
    theme_minimal()
}

ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc22, "# of formal partnerships established"))
```

### **RHCC23** - \# of Advocacy Activities

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(dat %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc23_advocacy), !is.na(rhcc23_mob), !is.na(rhcc23_training)) %>%
            group_by(year) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            dplyr::select(year,rhcc23_advocacy,rhcc23_mob,rhcc23_training) %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc23_advocacy,rhcc23_mob,rhcc23_training), 
                         names_to = "Type", names_prefix = "rhcc23_", 
                         values_to = "rhcc23") %>%
            mutate(`Type` = factor(`Type`, levels = c("advocacy", "mob","training"), 
                                   labels = c("Advocacy", "Community Mobilization","Training")))) +
    geom_bar(aes(y = rhcc23, x = year, alpha = `Type`),
             position = "stack", stat = "identity", fill = "#F2AD00") +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Advocacy" = 1, "Community Mobilization" = 0.7, "Training" = 0.4)) +
    labs(fill = "", x ="Year", y = "# of advocacy activities", alpha = "Type") +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.3,0.3), breaks = c(1,2)) +
    theme_minimal()
)
```

## Row

### **RHCC24** - \# of Rotary Members Positioned in Local Decision Making Groups or Advisory Bodies

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc24, "# of Rotary members positioned in local\ndecision-making groups or advisory bodies"))
```

### **RHCC25** - \# of Advocacy Activities Covered by Media

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(dat %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc25_pub), !is.na(rhcc25_media), !is.na(rhcc25_soc)) %>%
            group_by(year) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            dplyr::select(year,rhcc25_pub,rhcc25_soc,rhcc25_media) %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc25_pub,rhcc25_soc,rhcc25_media), 
                         names_to = "Type", names_prefix = "rhcc25_", 
                         values_to = "rhcc25") %>%
            mutate(`Type` = factor(`Type`, levels = c("pub","soc","media"), 
                                   labels = c("Articles", "Social media\n(Instagram/Faceboook)","Media coverage\n(radio/newspaper)")))) +
    geom_bar(aes(y = rhcc25, x = year, alpha = `Type`),
             position = "stack", stat = "identity", fill = "#F2AD00") +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Articles" = 1, "Social media\n(Instagram/Faceboook)" = 0.7, "Media coverage\n(radio/newspaper)" = 0.4)) +
    labs(fill = "", x ="Year", y = "# of advocacy activities covered by media", alpha = "Type") +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.3,0.3), breaks = c(1,2)) +
    theme_minimal()
)
```

## Row

### **RHCC26** - \# of Rotary Members Trained on Use of a Community Scorecard

```{r}
ggplotly(
   ggplot(dat %>%
            filter(!is.na(rhcc26_nonmembers_f), !is.na(rhcc26_nonmembers_m), 
                   !is.na(rhcc26_rotaractors_f), !is.na(rhcc26_rotarians_f),
                   !is.na(rhcc26_rotarians_m)) %>%
            group_by(year) %>%
            filter(row_number()==n()) %>%
            dplyr::select(year,rhcc26_nonmembers_f,rhcc26_nonmembers_m,
                          rhcc26_rotarians_f,rhcc26_rotarians_m,rhcc26_rotaractors_f) %>%
            pivot_longer(cols = c(rhcc26_nonmembers_f,rhcc26_nonmembers_m,
                          rhcc26_rotarians_f,rhcc26_rotarians_m,rhcc26_rotaractors_f), 
                         names_to = c("RHCC6","Type","Gender"), 
                         names_pattern ="(.+)_(.+)_(.+)", 
                         values_to = "rhcc26") %>%
            mutate(Type = factor(Type, levels = c("rotarians","nonmembers","rotaractors"), 
                                   labels = c("Rotarians", "Non-members","Rotaractors")),
                   Gender = factor(Gender, levels = c("m","f"), 
                                   labels = c("Male", "Female")),
                   year = ifelse(year==1,"Year 1", "Year 2"))) +
    geom_bar(aes(y = rhcc26, x = Type, alpha = Gender),
             position = "stack", stat = "identity", fill = "#F2AD00") +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Female" = 1, "Male" = 0.7)) +
    labs(fill = "", x ="", y = "# of Rotary members trained on use of a Community Scorecard", alpha = "Gender") +
    ggforce::facet_wrap_paginate(~year) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 9))
)
```

### **RHCC27** - \# of RHCC Districts Reporting on a Community Scorecard

```{r}
ggplotly(bar_plot(dat, rhcc27, "# of districts reporting on a community scorecard"))
```

# Zambia

```{r}
setwd("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/") #UPDATE HERE AND FIRST CHUNK FOR EACH COUNTRY TAB

setwd("MNTD/Projects/RHCC (Rotary)/Dashboard/data/clean/zambia/")

#RHCC1 summary datasets
#rhcc1_all <- read_csv("data/clean/rhcc1.csv")
rhcc1_mort <- read_csv("rhcc1_mort.csv")
rhcc1_hosp <- read_csv("rhcc1_hosp.csv")
rhcc1_hosp_monthly <- read_csv("rhcc1_hosp_monthly.csv")

#RHCC2 summary dataset
rhcc2_plotting_dat <- read_csv("rhcc2_u5.csv")

#RHCC5
deployment_dat <- read_csv("rhcc5.csv")

#RHCC training datasets 
training_data_grp_march <- read_csv("training_data_summary_march26.csv")

#RHCC 9 dataset
sick_kids_plotting_dat <- read_csv("rhcc9.csv")

#RHCC 11 dataset
commodity_dat <- read_csv("rhcc11.csv") %>%
  dplyr::select(district, zinc:period)

#RHCC 13 dataset
combined_rep <- read_csv("rhcc13.csv")

#palette = wes_palette("Darjeeling1")[c(1:3,5)]
cols = c("Chilubi" = pathcolors$DkTeal, "Chisamba" = pathcolors$Purple, "Kasama" = pathcolors$PtlTeal,
         "Luwingu" = pathcolors$PtlGreen, "Ngabwe" = pathcolors$Green, "Mungwi" = "royalblue4")
```

## Row

### **RHCC1** - Under-5 Mortality per 1,000 Under 5 Population. Under 5 Population is being used as an alternative to the WHO live-births method of calculation to allow for up-to-date burden tracking from monthly surveillance data.

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
rhcc1_mort_plot = rhcc1_mort %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_col(aes(x = disease, y = u5_mort_u5_pop, fill = district),
           position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Disease", y = "Under-5 deaths per 1,000 children under 5", fill = "District") +
  # scale_fill_manual(values = c(pathcolors$Purple, pathcolors$Teal),
  #                   breaks = c("2024", "2025")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  theme_minimal() +
  facet_wrap(vars(year)) +
  coord_flip()

ggplotly(rhcc1_mort_plot)
```

## Row

### **RHCC1.1** - Under-5 Hospitalizations per 1,000 Under 5

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# rhcc1_hosp_plot = rhcc1_hosp %>%
#   ggplot() +
#   geom_col(aes(x = district, y = u5_hospitalizations_u5_pop, fill = as.factor(year)),
#            position = "dodge") +
#   labs(x = "District", y = "Under 5 Hospitalizations per 1,000 Under 5 Population", fill = "Year") +
#   scale_fill_manual(values = c(pathcolors$Purple, pathcolors$Red),
#                     breaks = c("2024", "2025")) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   theme(text = element_text(size = 14)) +
#   facet_wrap(vars(disease)) +
#   coord_flip()
# 
# ggplotly(rhcc1_hosp_plot)

# rhcc1_hosp_plot_monthly = rhcc1_hosp_monthly %>%
#   ggplot() +
#   geom_line(aes(x = period, y = u5_hosp_u5_pop, color = disease), lwd = 0.8) +
#   labs(x = "Date", y = "Under 5 Hospitalizations per 1,000 Under 5 Population", color = "Disease") +
#   scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   theme(text = element_text(size = 14), legend.position = "bottom",
#         axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 70)) +
#   facet_wrap(vars(district), nrow = 1)

rhcc1_hosp_plot_monthly = rhcc1_hosp_monthly %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = u5_hosp_u5_pop, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Under-5 hospitalizations per 1,000 children under 5", color = "Disease") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "6 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm")) +
  facet_wrap(vars(disease), nrow = 1) +
  ylim(c(0,5))

ggplotly(rhcc1_hosp_plot_monthly)

```

## Row

### **RHCC2** - % of *Malaria* Cases Managed in the Community

```{r}
#Malaria
rhcc2_plot_malaria <-rhcc2_plotting_dat %>%
  filter(disease == "Malaria") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x = period, y = pct_community, color = district), lwd = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "% of malaria cases managed in the community", color = "District") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 months", limits = c(as.Date("2025-06-01"),as.Date("2026-01-01")), date_labels =  "%m/%Y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
```
